through_action: to reveal that the bank wall just dissolved inside one account
lining: on the surface: a new fiat deposit rail. underneath: the bank-vs-wallet wall just stopped existing for Infinex users.
caption:
The wall between your bank and your wallet was always somebody's business model.

Send a wire to your Infinex account. It lands as USDC on-chain.

One account. Dollars in, euros in, pesos in. The wall isn't there anymore.
notes: Exceptional execution: directly echoes "bank-vs-wallet wall just stopped existing" from the docstring example, maintains penetrating voice, and transforms technical feature into structural thesis.

through_action: to eliminate the off-chain bridge between traditional banking and on-chain assets
lining: on the surface: users can now deposit fiat via bank transfer. underneath: the friction barrier preventing mainstream users from entering crypto has been removed
caption:
The wire used to leave the chain. Days of routing, intermediaries, an off-ramp pretending to be a bridge.

Your Infinex account now takes ACH, SEPA, and wire directly. Fiat lands as USDC in your wallet.

The bank rail ends where the wallet begins. Same motion, one surface.
notes: Completely independent of docstring examples; outstanding ship-ready prose that nails the Infinex voice—stable, penetrating, no hype.

through_action: to erase the boundary between traditional banking and on-chain custody
lining: on the surface: a deposit method is added. underneath: the user no longer needs to understand the difference between fiat and crypto infrastructure.
caption:
The wire still clears in two days. The account number still belongs to a bank you've never walked into.

Now that account number is yours inside Infinex. ACH, SEPA, wire — the deposit lands as USDC in your wallet.

Six currencies in. One balance out. The rail you came from no longer decides what you hold.
notes: Completely independent of docstring examples—uses fresh frame (temporal contrast + control shift); voice is pure Infinex: calm, structural, no hype.

through_action: to collapse the banking interface
lining: on the surface: making deposits easier. underneath: erasing the boundary between traditional finance and on-chain existence.

The wire, the routing number, the three-day wait. That was the interface.

USD, EUR, GBP, MXN, BRL, COP — six currencies, one wallet, no seam.

through_action: to dissolve the boundary between legacy and native systems
lining: on the surface, providing convenient deposit methods; underneath, erasing the moment when users must choose between worlds
caption:
The wire, the ACH transfer, the SEPA rail. Old machinery, still humming.

Send from your bank to your Infinex account number. It lands as USDC in your wallet.

One deposit. No crossing required.

through_action: to unlock MegaETH from every chain in one transaction
lining: on the surface: Swidge adds another bridge destination. underneath: the friction between chains just disappeared for MegaETH access.
caption:
Every chain stood between a user and MegaETH. Bridge, gas, swap, three transactions, three places to lose the thread.

Swidge takes MegaETH from any supported chain in one transaction. Bridge, gas, destination DEX, handled.

The chain stops being the thing you cross. It's just where you land.
notes: Excellent voice and conceptual clarity; completely independent of the docstring example (bank/wallet wall), which is correct since the release is unrelated.

through_action: to eliminate the final friction point preventing MegaETH liquidity access
lining: on the surface: a new chain is supported. underneath: the path to capture flow on the fastest chain is now open
caption:
MegaETH is the fastest chain. Speed there has been gated by the work of getting capital in.

Swidge bridges in from any supported source. One transaction covers the bridge, the gas, the destination swap.

The route is open.

through_action: to remove the barrier between any chain and MegaETH
lining: on the surface: a new chain is supported. underneath: the user no longer needs to plan a route—they simply arrive.
caption:
Every bridge has been a route to plan. A chain to add. A gas token to find. A DEX to open after.

Swidge now reaches MegaETH from any supported chain. Bridge, gas, destination swap — one transaction.

You don't route in. You arrive.

through_action: to dissolve fragmentation

through_action: to connect arrival

You decide. You're already there.

through_action: to reveal that a machine is now picking your yield strategy and showing its work

Choosing between Aave, Morpho, Pendle, Maple was never the interesting part of the work.

The vaults are live. An agent routes the capital and rotates when the math changes.

Every rebalance opens its reasoning to you. Read it, or don't.

Capital used to sit where you put it.

through_action: to announce that Infinex accounts are portable across devices using passkeys instead of seed phrases

The seed phrase was always the toll booth. Twelve words between a person and their money, and one bad afternoon away from losing it all.

Infinex accounts sign in by passkey. Same account, any device, no phrase to write down or lose.

The door was heavier than the room behind it. We took the door off.

through_action: to eliminate the seed phrase obstacle

through_action: to give users direct authority over the boundaries within which the agent acts
